instruction: Walk into the kitchen area. Walk by the sink and oven. Walk straight into the hallway. turn right into the little room. Turn left and walk into the bedroom. Stop by the corner of the bed.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk into the kitchen area. Walk by the sink and oven. Walk straight into the hallway. turn right into the little room. Turn left and walk into the bedroom. Stop by the corner of the bed. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
I can see a kitchen area to the right (with what appears to be counters and appliances) and a living room to the left. Let me look around to get a full picture.

The kitchen area is ahead and to the right. I can see the kitchen counters/appliances in the "ahead" view on the right side. Let me move toward the kitchen area. The kitchen with the island and appliances is ahead-right.

Now I'm in the kitchen area. I can see the kitchen island, counters, and appliances on the right (the dark area with the oven/stove). There's a hallway visible on the right side. Let me continue walking through the kitchen, passing the sink and oven. Let me turn slightly right toward the kitchen appliances and hallway.

Now I can clearly see the kitchen with the oven/stove on the right (dark area with cabinets below). This is the sink and oven area. There's a hallway/opening visible ahead on the left side. Let me walk forward past the kitchen counter, keeping the oven on my right. Let me turn slightly left to head down the walkway.

I'm now walking alongside the kitchen. I can see the stove/oven on the right (with the cooktop). There's an opening/doorway ahead on the left side that likely leads to the hallway. Let me continue forward through the kitchen.

I'm getting close to the end of the kitchen. I can see the refrigerator on the left and there's an opening/hallway to the left. The center clearance is getting low (1.4m) meaning I'm approaching a wall. There's a doorway/opening on the left. Let me look around to find the hallway path.

Looking at the views: to the right (+90°) there's an opening/doorway leading out of the kitchen — this looks like it could lead to the hallway. The instruction says "Walk straight into the hallway." Ahead there's a wall (only 1.4m). The right view shows a doorway opening.

Let me move forward a little more then turn right into that opening which appears to be the hallway.

There's a doorway/opening on the left (I can see through to another room). The center is a wall now (1.2m). I've passed the sink and oven. Now I need to walk into the hallway. There's an opening to the left. Let me turn left to check that opening.

Now I can see down the kitchen the other way — there's the refrigerator ahead on the right, and an opening/hallway ahead. There's a passage that appears to continue straight. Let me move forward to reach the end of the kitchen where the hallway is.

I see the refrigerator ahead. There's a passage/hallway that continues. It looks like there's an opening to the left of the fridge and the space continues. The clearances are getting tight. Let me look around to understand the layout better here.

Now I understand: the oven/stove is to the right (+90°). The sink is to the left (+270°). Ahead there's a passage past the refrigerator that continues — this is likely the hallway. The instruction: "Walk by the sink and oven. Walk straight into the hallway."

Ahead past the refrigerator, there's a doorway/darker passage. Let me move forward toward it. I need to navigate carefully — I'll move forward slightly and adjust.

I'm near the refrigerator. There's a passage/hallway continuing ahead, past the fridge. I can see a doorway ahead. The right side is more open (1.2m). Let me turn slightly right and continue forward into the hallway.
Now the center is open (2.3m) — I can see a passage ahead. There's an oven on the right side. The path continues forward. This is the hallway direction. Let me continue forward.
I'm in a narrow galley area (looks like a butler's pantry/kitchen with a sink on the left). The passage continues ahead with a doorway. Let me continue forward toward the doorway/hallway ahead.
I see a doorway ahead leading into another room/hallway. There's an opening in the center ahead. Let me continue forward through this doorway.
I'm right at the doorway now. Through it I can see another space that looks like it could be a hallway or laundry area. Let me pass through the doorway.

Ahead I can see through a doorway there's a bathroom (with a sink/vanity) on the left, and a hallway continuing to the right with another room visible ahead. This is the hallway area. The instruction says "Walk straight into the hallway. turn right into the little room."

Let me move forward into this hallway area first.

I'm at the doorway now. To the left is a bathroom (sink/vanity). To the right there's a hallway continuing and I can see into a room. The instruction says walk straight into the hallway, then turn right into the little room. Let me move forward into this junction and look right.

Now I have a clear picture. Ahead is the bathroom (with sink). To the right (+90°) there's a room with windows and furniture — this looks like a sitting room/little room. To the left (+270°) is more hallway.

The instruction: "Walk straight into the hallway. turn right into the little room. Turn left and walk into the bedroom."

I'm in the hallway. Turning right leads into a little room. Let me turn right and go into that room.
I'm now facing the little room. It has windows, a sofa/chair on the left, and appears to be a small sitting room or study. Let me walk into it.

I'm in the little room now. I can see a sofa on the left, windows in the center. The instruction says "Turn left and walk into the bedroom." Let me look around to find the bedroom which should be to my left.

Looking at the left view (+270°), I can see through a doorway into what looks like a bedroom (there's a bed/furniture visible in the far room). The instruction says "Turn left and walk into the bedroom." 

The left view shows an opening on the right side (2.6m clearance) leading to a room with what appears to be a bed. Let me turn left and head toward that bedroom.

I can see doorways ahead leading into a bedroom (I can see a bed on the right through the doorway). The open path is to the right (2.6m). Let me turn slightly right to head through the doorway into the bedroom.

Now I can see the bedroom. There's a bed on the right side (I can see the mattress/bed at the bottom right), and there appear to be mirrors/closets on the left. Let me continue forward into the bedroom toward the bed.
I can see the bedroom clearly now with a bed visible in the center/left area (through what appears to be a mirror or opening). Let me look around to orient myself to the bed.

Interesting. To my left (+270°) are white closets/wardrobes. To my right (+90°) is a sitting area with a chair and shelving. Ahead I see the bedroom with a bed reflected in a mirror. The actual bed seems to be to my left-ahead. Let me examine the ahead view more — there's a bed visible. It seems the bed is in the room to the left through the mirror/doorway.

Actually the "ahead" view shows a mirror reflecting a bedroom with a bed. Let me move forward and see the layout better. The bed appears to be through the opening on the left side of the ahead view.
I can see the bed on the right side through a doorway/opening. There's a bed with pillows visible. The bed is to my right ahead. Let me turn right and go toward the bed.
Now I can clearly see the bed through the doorway ahead-right. It's a bed with dark/patterned bedding. I'm at a doorway. Let me turn a bit right and go through toward the bed.

Now I'm at the entrance to the bedroom. I can clearly see the bed ahead with the headboard and bedding. The instruction says "Stop by the corner of the bed." The bed is ahead of me. Let me walk toward the corner of the bed.
I'm now inside the bedroom. The bed is right in front of me, slightly to the left. The corner of the bed (the foot corner near me) is close. Let me move forward a bit toward the corner of the bed.

I'm now right by the corner of the bed. The bed is directly in front of me and to the left — I can see the foot/corner of the bed with the yellow throw and pillows. I'm well within 3 meters of the corner of the bed. 

I've completed the instruction: walked through the kitchen (past sink and oven), through the hallway, turned right into the little room, turned left into the bedroom, and now I'm by the corner of the bed. Let me stop here.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 43 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 57, "tool_calls_remaining": 43}

text: Let me verify my placement. The final clause is "Stop by the corner of the bed." I'm right next to the bed's corner. Let me look around to confirm I'm at the best spot.

I'm confirmed to be in the bedroom, standing right at the corner/foot of the bed. The bed is directly to my left-ahead with its foot corner clearly close to me (the yellow throw and pillows visible). I've successfully:
- Walked into the kitchen, passed the sink and oven
- Walked straight into the hallway
- Turned right into the little room
- Turned left and walked into the bedroom
- Now stopped by the corner of the bed

This is the correct endpoint. Let me confirm the STOP.
